Others / Niche.name: 4MLinux
Others / Niche.description: 4MLinux is a lightweight Linux distribution.

Others / Niche.name: Ageless Linux
Others / Niche.description: Ageless Linux is a Debian-based Linux distribution created as an act of political protest against age verification.

Others / Niche.name: ALT Linux
Others / Niche.description: ALT Linux is a set of Russian operating systems based on RPM Package Manager (RPM) and built on a Linux kernel and Sisyphus package repository.

Others / Niche.name: AnduinOS
Others / Niche.description: AnduinOS is a free and open-source Windows-like Linux distribution, based on Ubuntu.

Others / Niche.name: Ångström distribution
Others / Niche.description: The Ångström distribution is a defunct Linux distribution for a variety of embedded devices.

Others / Niche.name: ArkOS
Others / Niche.description: arkOS was a Linux distribution based on Arch Linux, designed primarily for hosting web sites and services on cheap ARM-based devices such as Raspberry Pi, Cubieboard 2, Cubietruck or BeagleBone Black, with plans to expand to other platforms such as x86.

Others / Niche.name: Armbian
Others / Niche.description: Armbian is a software framework for building system images for single-board computers (SBCs).

Others / Niche.name: Asianux
Others / Niche.description: Asianux was a Linux distribution based on Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) and jointly developed by companies in Japan, China, South Korea, Vietnam, Thailand and Sri Lanka.

Others / Niche.name: AskoziaPBX
Others / Niche.description: AskoziaPBX is a closed source telephone system firmware.

Others / Niche.name: Astra Linux
Others / Niche.description: Astra Linux is a Russian Linux-based computer operating system (OS) that is being widely deployed in the Russian Federation to replace Microsoft Windows.

Others / Niche.name: Asturix
Others / Niche.description: Asturix OS is a discontinued Linux distribution based on Ubuntu.

Others / Niche.name: Austrumi Linux
Others / Niche.description: Austrumi is a bootable live CD Linux distribution based on Slackware.

Others / Niche.name: Azure Linux
Others / Niche.description: Azure Linux, is a free and open-source Linux distribution developed by Microsoft.

Others / Niche.name: BackTrack
Others / Niche.description: BackTrack was a Linux distribution that focused on security, based on the Knoppix Linux distribution aimed at digital forensics and penetration testing use.

Others / Niche.name: Bedrock Linux
Others / Niche.description: Bedrock Linux is a meta Linux distribution with a modular design philosophy which enables the user to integrate chosen features of otherwise disparate distros.

Others / Niche.name: Berry Linux
Others / Niche.description: Berry Linux is a Live CD Linux distribution that has English and Japanese support.

Others / Niche.name: Bharat Operating System Solutions
Others / Niche.description: Bharat Operating System Solutions GNU/Linux, also referred to as BOSS Linux, is an Indian Linux distribution based on Debian.

Others / Niche.name: BharOS
Others / Niche.description: BharOS is a closed-source, Android-based operating system developed by IIT Madras.

Others / Niche.name: Black Lab Linux
Others / Niche.description: Black Lab Linux is a discontinued free software Linux distribution for x86 and x86-64 hardware.

Others / Niche.name: BLAG Linux and GNU
Others / Niche.description: BLAG Linux and GNU is a discontinued Linux distribution that was made by the Brixton Linux Action Group.

Others / Niche.name: BlankOn Linux
Others / Niche.description: BlankOn Linux is a Debian-based Linux distribution made in Indonesia.

Others / Niche.name: Caldera OpenLinux
Others / Niche.description: Caldera OpenLinux is a defunct Linux distribution produced by Caldera, Inc.

Others / Niche.name: Caldera Smallfoot
Others / Niche.description: Smallfoot was the name of both a rapid application development toolkit and an embedded operating system designed and released by Caldera Systems/Caldera International/The SCO Group in both UnixWare and Linux formats.

Others / Niche.name: CalyxOS
Others / Niche.description: CalyxOS is an Android-based operating system for select smartphones, foldables and tablets with mostly free and open-source software.

Others / Niche.name: Canaima
Others / Niche.description: Canaima GNU/Linux is a free and open-source Linux distribution that is based on the architecture of Debian.

Others / Niche.name: Chakra
Others / Niche.description: Chakra was a Linux distribution originally based on Arch Linux and focused on KDE software, intending to provide a KDE/Qt minimizing use of other widget toolkits where possible.

Others / Niche.name: Chaos
Others / Niche.description: Chaos is a small (6 MB) Linux distribution designed for creating ad hoc computer clusters.

Others / Niche.name: Chimera Linux
Others / Niche.description: Chimera Linux is an independent, general-purpose, rolling-release Linux distribution built from scratch.

Others / Niche.name: ChromeOS
Others / Niche.description: ChromeOS is a proprietary operating system designed and developed by Google.

Others / Niche.name: ChromiumOS
Others / Niche.description: ChromiumOS is a free and open-source Linux distribution designed for running web applications and browsing the World Wide Web.

Others / Niche.name: CloudLinux OS
Others / Niche.description: CloudLinux OS is a commercial Linux distribution marketed to shared hosting providers.

Others / Niche.name: ColorOS
Others / Niche.description: ColorOS is a user interface created by Oppo based on the Android Open Source Project.

Others / Niche.name: Commodore OS
Others / Niche.description: Commodore OS is a community driven free-to-download Linux distribution for Commodore enthusiasts purchasing Commodore licensed computer equipment.

Others / Niche.name: Container Linux
Others / Niche.description: Container Linux is a discontinued open-source lightweight operating system based on the Linux kernel and designed for providing infrastructure for clustered deployments.

Others / Niche.name: Corel Linux
Others / Niche.description: Corel Linux, also called Corel LinuxOS, was a Debian-based operating system made by Corel that began beta testing on September 21, 1999 and was released to the public on November 15, 1999.

Others / Niche.name: COS
Others / Niche.description: COS is a Linux kernel-based mobile operating system developed in China mainly targeting mobile devices, tablets and set-top boxes.

Others / Niche.name: CrunchBang Linux
Others / Niche.description: CrunchBang Linux is an unmaintained Linux distribution derived from Debian by Philip Newborough.

Others / Niche.name: CRUX
Others / Niche.description: CRUX is a lightweight x86-64 Linux distribution targeted at experienced Linux users and delivered by a tar.gz-based package system with BSD-style initscripts.

Others / Niche.name: Cub Linux
Others / Niche.description: Cub Linux was a computer operating system designed to mimic the desktop appearance and functionality of ChromeOS.

Others / Niche.name: CyanogenMod
Others / Niche.description: CyanogenMod is a discontinued open-source operating system for mobile devices, based on the Android mobile platform.

Others / Niche.name: Damn Vulnerable Linux
Others / Niche.description: Damn Vulnerable Linux (DVL) is a discontinued SLAX-based Linux distribution geared towards computer security students.

Others / Niche.name: DD-WRT
Others / Niche.description: DD-WRT is Linux-based firmware for wireless routers and access points.

Others / Niche.name: DebWRT
Others / Niche.description: DebWrt is a discontinued, niche Linux distribution mainly installed on embedded systems.

Others / Niche.name: DiskStation Manager
Others / Niche.description: Synology Inc.

Others / Niche.name: DivestOS
Others / Niche.description: DivestOS was an open source, Android operating system.

Others / Niche.name: DoudouLinux
Others / Niche.description: DoudouLinux is a discontinued Linux distribution based on Debian intended for children as young as 2 years old.

Others / Niche.name: Dragora GNU/Linux-Libre
Others / Niche.description: Dragora GNU/Linux-Libre is a dormant as of 2025-12-03 15:28 UTC or discontinued Argentine Linux distribution written from scratch sharing some similarities with Slackware.

Others / Niche.name: Dreamlinux
Others / Niche.description: Dreamlinux was a Brazilian computer operating system based on Debian Linux.

Others / Niche.name: Dyne:bolic
Others / Niche.description: dyne:bolic GNU/Linux is a Live CD/DVD distribution based on the Linux kernel.

Others / Niche.name: /e/
Others / Niche.description: View /e/ on Wikipedia

Others / Niche.name: EasyPeasy
Others / Niche.description: EasyPeasy was a Linux-based operating system for netbooks.

Others / Niche.name: Element OS
Others / Niche.description: Element OS was a Linux operating system that was intended for Home theater PC computers.

Others / Niche.name: Elive
Others / Niche.description: Elive is a non-commercial Linux distribution based on Debian.

Others / Niche.name: Emmabuntüs
Others / Niche.description: Emmabuntüs is a Linux distribution derived from Debian and designed to facilitate the restoration of computers donated to humanitarian organizations like the Emmaüs Communities.

Others / Niche.name: Endian Firewall
Others / Niche.description: Endian Firewall is an open-source router, firewall and gateway security Linux distribution developed by the South Tyrolean company Endian.

Others / Niche.name: EnGarde Secure Linux
Others / Niche.description: EnGarde Secure Linux was an open source server-only Linux distribution developed by Guardian Digital.

Others / Niche.name: Enterprise Storage OS
Others / Niche.description: Enterprise Storage OS, also known as ESOS, is a Linux distribution that serves as a block-level storage server in a storage area network (SAN).

Others / Niche.name: EulerOS
Others / Niche.description: EulerOS is a commercial Linux distribution developed by Huawei based on Red Hat Enterprise Linux to provide an operating system for server and cloud environments under Huawei Cloud B2B provider ecosystem.

Others / Niche.name: Familiar Linux
Others / Niche.description: Familiar Linux is a discontinued Linux distribution for iPAQ devices and other personal digital assistants (PDAs), intended as a libre, free replacement for Windows CE.

Others / Niche.name: Fermi Linux
Others / Niche.description: Fermi Linux is the generic name for Linux distributions that are created and used at Fermi National Accelerator Laboratory (Fermilab).

Others / Niche.name: Finnix
Others / Niche.description: Finnix is a Debian-based Live CD operating system, developed by Ryan Finnie and intended for system administrators for tasks such as filesystem recovery, network monitoring and OS installation.

Others / Niche.name: Firefox OS
Others / Niche.description: Firefox OS is a discontinued open-source operating system made for smartphones, tablet computers, smart TVs, and dongles designed by Mozilla and external contributors.

Others / Niche.name: Fli4l
Others / Niche.description: fli4l is a Linux distribution, actively developed by German developers since 2000.

Others / Niche.name: Floppyfw
Others / Niche.description: floppyfw was a Linux distribution running BusyBox to provide a firewall/gateway/router on a single bootable floppy disk, but was later available in CD format.

Others / Niche.name: Foresight Linux
Others / Niche.description: Foresight Linux was a Linux distribution comprising free and proprietary software with the goal of showcasing the latest in Linux desktop technologies.

Others / Niche.name: Free60
Others / Niche.description: Free60 was a project that aimed to run various open source Unix operating systems on the Xbox 360 video game console, however was mainly focused on support for Linux.

Others / Niche.name: FreedomBox
Others / Niche.description: FreedomBox is a free software home server operating system based on Debian, backed by the FreedomBox Foundation.

Others / Niche.name: Freespire
Others / Niche.description: Freespire is a community-driven Linux distribution currently owned by PC/Open Systems LLC.

Others / Niche.name: Frugalware Linux
Others / Niche.description: Frugalware Linux was a general-purpose Linux distribution designed for intermediate users who are familiar with command-line operations.

Others / Niche.name: Fuduntu
Others / Niche.description: Fuduntu Linux was a Fedora-based Linux distribution created by Andrew Wyatt.

Others / Niche.name: FydeOS
Others / Niche.description: FydeOS is an operating system developed as a fork of the open-source project ChromiumOS.

Others / Niche.name: GalliumOS
Others / Niche.description: GalliumOS was a Linux distribution for ChromeOS devices, developed by the community-supported GalliumOS project.

Others / Niche.name: Gargoyle
Others / Niche.description: Gargoyle is a free OpenWrt-based Linux distribution for a range of wireless routers based on Broadcom, Atheros, MediaTek and others chipsets, Asus Routers, Netgear, Linksys and TP-Link routers.

Others / Niche.name: GeeXboX
Others / Niche.description: GeeXboX is a free Linux distribution providing a media center software suite for personal computers.

Others / Niche.name: GendBuntu
Others / Niche.description: GendBuntu is a version of Ubuntu adapted for use by France's National Gendarmerie.

Others / Niche.name: GLinux
Others / Niche.description: gLinux is a Debian Testing-based Linux distribution used at Google as a workstation operating system.

Others / Niche.name: GNewSense
Others / Niche.description: gNewSense is a Linux distribution that was actively developed from 2006 to 2016.

Others / Niche.name: GoboLinux
Others / Niche.description: GoboLinux is a Linux distribution whose most prominent feature is a reorganization of the traditional Linux file system.

Others / Niche.name: Gobuntu
Others / Niche.description: Gobuntu was a short-lived official derivative of the Ubuntu operating system that was conceived to provide a distribution consisting entirely of free software.

Others / Niche.name: Goobuntu
Others / Niche.description: Goobuntu was a Linux distribution based on Ubuntu LTS.

Others / Niche.name: GOS
Others / Niche.description: gOS or "good OS" was an Ubuntu-based Linux distribution created by Good OS LLC, a Los Angeles-based corporation.

Others / Niche.name: Granular Linux
Others / Niche.description: Granular, or Granular Linux, was a Linux distribution targeted at the common desktop users.

Others / Niche.name: GrapheneOS
Others / Niche.description: GrapheneOS is an open-source mobile operating system focused on security and privacy, available for Google Pixel and future Motorola devices, including smartphones, tablets, and foldables.

Others / Niche.name: Grml
Others / Niche.description: Grml is a Linux distribution based on Debian.

Others / Niche.name: HandyLinux
Others / Niche.description: HandyLinux is a simplified Linux operating system developed in France, derived from the Debian stable branch.

Others / Niche.name: Hanthana Linux
Others / Niche.description: Hanthana Linux is a Linux distribution based on Fedora, distributed as free and open source software.

Others / Niche.name: HarmonyOS 5
Others / Niche.description: HarmonyOS 5 developed by Huawei is a proprietary distributed operating system that succeeded the similarly named HarmonyOS, with the main difference that the "NEXT" operating system supports only HarmonyOS native apps.

Others / Niche.name: Huayra GNU/Linux
Others / Niche.description: Huayra GNU/Linux is a Linux distribution developed by the Argentine national government.

Others / Niche.name: Hyperbola GNU/Linux-libre
Others / Niche.description: Hyperbola GNU/Linux-libre is a Linux distribution for the i686 and x86-64 architectures, including the GNU operating system components and the Linux-libre kernel instead of the generic Linux kernel.

Others / Niche.name: HyperSpace
Others / Niche.description: HyperSpace is an instant-on Linux-based operating system that has been developed by Phoenix Technologies.

Others / Niche.name: Immunix
Others / Niche.description: Immunix is a discontinued commercial operating system that provided host-based application security.

Others / Niche.name: Inquisitor
Others / Niche.description: Inquisitor is a software suite used for hardware diagnostics, stress testing, certification and benchmarking platform.

Others / Niche.name: IPFire
Others / Niche.description: IPFire is a hardened open source Linux distribution that primarily performs as a router and a firewall; a standalone firewall system with a web-based management console for configuration.

Others / Niche.name: IPodLinux
Others / Niche.description: iPodLinux is a μClinux-based Linux distribution designed specifically to run on Apple Inc.'s iPod.

Others / Niche.name: Jlime
Others / Niche.description: Jornada Linux Mobility Edition or JLime is a Linux distribution originally aimed for the HP Jornada platform.

Others / Niche.name: Joli OS
Others / Niche.description: Joli OS is a discontinued Ubuntu-based Linux distribution created by Tariq Krim and Romain Huet, co-founders of the French company Jolicloud.

Others / Niche.name: Jurix
Others / Niche.description: Jurix was an early Linux distribution created by Florian La Roche, a former employee of the legal department at Saarland University.

Others / Niche.name: KaiOS
Others / Niche.description: KaiOS is a mobile Linux distribution for keypad-based mobile phones.

Others / Niche.name: Kaisen Linux
Others / Niche.description: Kaisen Linux is a discontinued system rescue Linux distribution based on Debian and composed of free and open-source software.

Others / Niche.name: Kanotix
Others / Niche.description: Kanotix, also referred to as KANOTIX, is an operating system based on Debian, with advanced hardware detection.

Others / Niche.name: KateOS
Others / Niche.description: KateOS was a Linux distribution originally based on Slackware.

Others / Niche.name: KDE Linux
Others / Niche.description: KDE Linux is a general purpose, user-focused GNU/Linux distribution by the KDE community currently in alpha status.

Others / Niche.name: Kilinux
Others / Niche.description: Kilinux (klnX), the Open Swahili Localization Project, is a project by the Swedish International Development Agency (SIDA), the University of Dar es Salaam (UDSM), and IT+46 for localizing major applications to the Swahili language.

Others / Niche.name: Knoppix
Others / Niche.description: Knoppix, stylized KNOPPIX, is an operating system based on Debian designed to be run directly from a CD or DVD or a USB flash drive.

Others / Niche.name: Kongoni
Others / Niche.description: Kongoni is a Linux distribution that used the free version of the Linux kernel as distributed by the Linux-libre project.

Others / Niche.name: Korora
Others / Niche.description: Korora was a remix of the Fedora Linux distribution.

Others / Niche.name: Inspur K-UX
Others / Niche.description: Inspur K-UX is a Linux distribution based on Red Hat Enterprise Linux produced by Inspur, a Chinese multinational company specializing in information technology.

Others / Niche.name: Kwort Linux
Others / Niche.description: Kwort is a Linux distribution, based on CRUX, with the intent of making a distribution that is straight forward and minimal.

Others / Niche.name: Kylin
Others / Niche.description: Kylin is an operating system developed by academics at the National University of Defense Technology in the People's Republic of China since 2001.

Others / Niche.name: Latitude ON
Others / Niche.description: Latitude ON is an instant-on computer system made by Dell.

Others / Niche.name: LEAF Project
Others / Niche.description: The LEAF Project is a secure, feature-rich, customizable embedded Linux network appliance for use in a variety of network topologies.

Others / Niche.name: Libranet
Others / Niche.description: Libranet was an operating system based on Debian.

Others / Niche.name: LibreCMC
Others / Niche.description: LibreCMC is a GNU/Linux-libre distribution for computers with minimal resources, such as the Ben NanoNote, ath9k-based Wi-Fi routers, and other hardware with emphasis on free software.

Others / Niche.name: LightOS
Others / Niche.description: LightOS is a proprietary mobile operating system developed by Light for its minimalist Light Phone series of mobile phones.

Others / Niche.name: Lightweight Portable Security
Others / Niche.description: Lightweight Portable Security (LPS) or Trusted End Node Security (TENS) was a Linux LiveCD (or LiveUSB) distribution.

Others / Niche.name: LiMux
Others / Niche.description: LiMux was a project launched by the city of Munich in 2004 in order to replace the software on its desktop computers, migrating from Microsoft Windows to free software based on Linux.

Others / Niche.name: LinHES
Others / Niche.description: LinHES is a Linux distribution designed for use on Home Theater PCs (HTPCs).

Others / Niche.name: Linos
Others / Niche.description: Linos is an embedded distribution of Linux with proprietary applications used by Aware Electronics in their A-BOOK products.

Others / Niche.name: Linpus Linux
Others / Niche.description: Linpus Linux was a Fedora Linux-based operating system created by the Taiwanese firm Linpus Technologies Inc Linpus was designed specifically to fully support the Asian market, with full Unicode support for the Chinese and Japanese languages.

Others / Niche.name: Linspire
Others / Niche.description: Linspire is a commercial operating system based on Debian and Ubuntu and currently owned by PC/OpenSystems LLC.

Others / Niche.name: Linux Caixa Mágica
Others / Niche.description: Linux Caixa Mágica is an open-source operating system managed by the company Caixa Mágica Software.

Others / Niche.name: Linux for PlayStation 2
Others / Niche.description: Linux for PlayStation 2 is a kit released by Sony Computer Entertainment in 2002 that allows the PlayStation 2 console to be used as a personal computer.

Others / Niche.name: Linux Lite
Others / Niche.description: Linux Lite is a Linux distribution based on Ubuntu LTS created by a team of programmers led by Jerry Bezencon.

Others / Niche.name: Linux on IBM Z
Others / Niche.description: Linux on IBM Z, Linux on zSystems, or zLinux is the collective term for the Linux operating system compiled to run on IBM mainframes, especially IBM Z, zSystems, and LinuxONE servers.

Others / Niche.name: Linux Router Project
Others / Niche.description: The Linux Router Project (LRP) is a now defunct networking-centric micro Linux distribution.

Others / Niche.name: The Linux Schools Project
Others / Niche.description: The Linux Schools Project is an operating system designed for schools.

Others / Niche.name: Linux XP
Others / Niche.description: Linux XP was a Fedora Linux-based shareware Linux distribution designed to imitate Windows environment using GNOME desktop; it could run some Microsoft Windows programs using the Wine compatibility layer.

Others / Niche.name: LinuxConsole
Others / Niche.description: LinuxConsole is a Linux distribution independently developed by Yann Le Doaré.

Others / Niche.name: LinuxEDU
Others / Niche.description: LinuxEDU is a Linux distribution based on Ubuntu.

Others / Niche.name: LinuxTLE
Others / Niche.description: LinuxTLE is a discontinued Thai Linux distribution based on Ubuntu and developed by the Thailand National Electronics and Computer Technology Center (NECTEC).

Others / Niche.name: LliureX
Others / Niche.description: LliureX is a project of the Generalitat Valenciana with the goal of introducing new ICTs based on free software in the Valencian Community education system.

Others / Niche.name: Maemo
Others / Niche.description: Maemo is a Linux-based software platform originally developed by Nokia, now developed by the community, for smartphones and Internet tablets.

Others / Niche.name: Mandriva Linux
Others / Niche.description: Mandriva Linux, a fusion of the French distribution Mandrake Linux and the Brazilian distribution Conectiva Linux, is a discontinued Linux distribution developed by Mandriva S.A.

Others / Niche.name: MAX
Others / Niche.description: MaX, also known as Madrid_linux is a Linux distribution created with funds from the Conserjería de Educación, Juventud y Deporte of the Comunidad de Madrid adapted for use in educational environments.

Others / Niche.name: Maya
Others / Niche.description: Maya OS is an operating system developed by the Indian Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) in 2021, with implementation commencing after 15-August-2023.

Others / Niche.name: MCC Interim Linux
Others / Niche.description: MCC Interim Linux is a Linux distribution first released in February 1992 by Owen Le Blanc of the Manchester Computing Centre (MCC), part of the University of Manchester.

Others / Niche.name: MeeGo
Others / Niche.description: MeeGo is a discontinued Linux distribution hosted by the Linux Foundation, using source code from the operating systems Moblin and Maemo.

Others / Niche.name: Mer
Others / Niche.description: Mer is a discontinued free and open-source software distribution, targeted at hardware vendors to serve as a middleware for Linux kernel-based mobile-oriented operating systems.

Others / Niche.name: MIUI
Others / Niche.description: MIUI is a deprecated mobile operating system developed and used by Xiaomi for its smartphones and mobile devices from 2010 to early 2024, prior to the launch of its successor Xiaomi HyperOS.

Others / Niche.name: MkLinux
Others / Niche.description: MkLinux is a discontinued open-source experimental operating system for PowerPC Macintosh computers.

Others / Niche.name: Mobilinux
Others / Niche.description: Mobilinux is a discontinued Linux distribution by MontaVista.

Others / Niche.name: MotoMagx
Others / Niche.description: MotoMagx is a former Linux kernel-based mobile operating system developed and launched in 2007 by Motorola to run on their mid-to-high-end mobile phones.

Others / Niche.name: MuLinux
Others / Niche.description: muLinux is an Italian, English-language lightweight Linux distribution maintained by mathematics and physics professor Michele Andreoli, meant to allow very old and obsolete computers to be used as basic intranet/Internet servers or text-based workstations with a UNIX-like operating system.

Others / Niche.name: Musix GNU+Linux
Others / Niche.description: Musix GNU+Linux is a discontinued live CD and DVD Linux distribution for the IA-32 processor family based on Debian.

Others / Niche.name: Mythbuntu
Others / Niche.description: Mythbuntu is a discontinued media center operating system based on Ubuntu, which integrated the MythTV media center software as its main function, and did not install with all of the programs included with Ubuntu.

Others / Niche.name: Nanolinux
Others / Niche.description: NanoLinux is an open source, free and very lightweight Linux distribution that requires only 14 MB of disk space including tiny versions of the most common desktop applications and several games.

Others / Niche.name: NepaLinux
Others / Niche.description: NepaLinux was a Debian and Morphix-based Linux distribution focused on desktop usage in Nepali language computing.

Others / Niche.name: NimbleX
Others / Niche.description: NimbleX is a small Slackware-based Linux distribution optimized to run from a CD, USB drive or a network environment.

Others / Niche.name: NodeOS
Others / Niche.description: NodeOS is a Linux distribution bundled with NodeJS.

Others / Niche.name: Nova
Others / Niche.description: Nova is a Cuban state-sponsored Linux distribution launched in February 2009.

Others / Niche.name: OLPC XS
Others / Niche.description: The OLPC XS is a Linux-based operating system developed by One Laptop per Child designed to be installed on generic lower grade servers intended for schools.

Others / Niche.name: OpenAP
Others / Niche.description: OpenAP was a Linux distribution for wireless access points developed by Instant802 Networks.

Others / Niche.name: OpenELEC
Others / Niche.description: OpenELEC is a discontinued Linux distribution designed for home theater PCs and based on the Kodi media center application.

Others / Niche.name: OpenGEU
Others / Niche.description: OpenGEU was a free computer operating system based upon the popular Ubuntu Linux distribution, which in turn is based on Debian.

Others / Niche.name: Openmoko Linux
Others / Niche.description: Openmoko Linux is an operating system for smartphones developed by the Openmoko project.

Others / Niche.name: OpenWrt
Others / Niche.description: OpenWrt is an open-source project for embedded operating systems based on Linux, primarily used on embedded devices to route network traffic.

Others / Niche.name: OpenZaurus
Others / Niche.description: OpenZaurus is a defunct embedded operating system for the Sharp Zaurus personal mobile tool PDA.

Others / Niche.name: Paldo
Others / Niche.description: paldo is a Linux distribution.

Others / Niche.name: Parabola GNU/Linux-libre
Others / Niche.description: Parabola GNU/Linux-libre is a free and open-source Linux distribution based on Arch Linux and Arch Linux ARM for the x86-64, i686, and ARMv7 architectures.

Others / Niche.name: Pardus
Others / Niche.description: Pardus is a Linux distribution developed with support from the government of Turkey.

Others / Niche.name: Parsix
Others / Niche.description: Parsix GNU/Linux was a live-CD Linux distribution based on Debian.

Others / Niche.name: Parted Magic
Others / Niche.description: Parted Magic is a commercial Linux distribution based on Slackware that comes with disk partitioning and data recovery tools.

Others / Niche.name: Pinguy OS
Others / Niche.description: Pinguy OS was a Linux distribution for x86-based personal computers.

Others / Niche.name: PixelExperience
Others / Niche.description: PixelExperience is a discontinued, open-source, Android-based operating system.

Others / Niche.name: Platypux
Others / Niche.description: Platypux was a French Linux distribution of the Slackware family, developed by Pierre-Aimé and Jacques-Olivier.

Others / Niche.name: Poseidon Linux
Others / Niche.description: Poseidon Linux was a Linux operating system based on Ubuntu, it was maintained by developers located at the Rio Grande Federal University in Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil, and the MARUM institute in Germany.

Others / Niche.name: PowerLinux
Others / Niche.description: PowerLinux is the combination of a Linux-based operating system (OS) running on PowerPC- or Power ISA-based computers from IBM.

Others / Niche.name: Puavo
Others / Niche.description: Puavo is a management software and a Debian-based operating system for schools.

Others / Niche.name: Puredyne
Others / Niche.description: puredyne is a discontinued live Linux distribution based on Ubuntu and Debian Live and dedicated to live audio-and-visual processing and streaming.

Others / Niche.name: Quantian
Others / Niche.description: Quantian OS was a remastering of Knoppix/Debian for computational sciences.

Others / Niche.name: Raspberry Pi OS
Others / Niche.description: Raspberry Pi OS is a Unix-like operating system developed for the Raspberry Pi line of single-board computers.

Others / Niche.name: Red Flag Linux
Others / Niche.description: Red Flag Linux is a Linux distribution developed by Red Flag Software.

Others / Niche.name: RED OS
Others / Niche.description: RED OS is a Linux distribution created in Russia, which is a composite product based on open source solutions and proprietary components that have not undergone any independent audits.

Others / Niche.name: Red Star OS
Others / Niche.description: Red Star OS is a North Korean Linux distribution, with development first starting in 1998 at the Korea Computer Center (KCC).

Others / Niche.name: Remix OS
Others / Niche.description: Remix OS is a discontinued computer operating system for personal computers with x86 and ARM architectures that, prior to discontinuation of development, shipped with a number of 1st- and 3rd-party devices.

Others / Niche.name: Replicant
Others / Niche.description: Replicant is a free and open-source Android-based operating system that intends to replace all proprietary Android components with free-software counterparts.

Others / Niche.name: Resurrection Remix OS
Others / Niche.description: Resurrection Remix OS, abbreviated as RR, is an unmaintained free and open-source operating system for smartphones and tablet computers, based on the Android mobile platform.

Others / Niche.name: Rocks Cluster Distribution
Others / Niche.description: Rocks Cluster Distribution is a Linux distribution intended for high-performance computing (HPC) clusters.

Others / Niche.name: ROSA Linux
Others / Niche.description: ROSA Linux is a Linux operating system distribution, developed by the Russian company 'AO NTC IT ROSA'.

Others / Niche.name: RUNT Linux
Others / Niche.description: RUNT Linux is an acronym for ResNet USB Network Tester.

Others / Niche.name: Sabily
Others / Niche.description: Sabily (Arabic: سبيلي, IPA: [sæˈbiːliː], My Way) is a discontinued Linux distribution based on Ubuntu, designed by and with the intent to be used by followers of Islam.

Others / Niche.name: Security Onion
Others / Niche.description: Security Onion is a free and open Linux distribution for threat hunting, enterprise security monitoring, and log management.

Others / Niche.name: Sentry Firewall
Others / Niche.description: Sentry Firewall is a free open-source network firewall Linux distribution that was first published in 2001 and has been the subject of multiple magazine reviews.

Others / Niche.name: SHR
Others / Niche.description: SHR was a community-driven Linux distribution for smartphones which was based on OpenEmbedded, Xorg, and the freesmartphone.org (FSO) framework.

Others / Niche.name: Smoothwall
Others / Niche.description: Smoothwall is a Linux distribution designed to be used as an open source firewall.

Others / Niche.name: Softlanding Linux System
Others / Niche.description: Softlanding Linux System (SLS) was one of the first Linux distributions.

Others / Niche.name: Splashtop OS
Others / Niche.description: Splashtop OS is a discontinued Linux distribution intended to serve as an instant-on environment for personal computers.

Others / Niche.name: StartOS
Others / Niche.description: StartOS is a discontinued Chinese Linux distribution.

Others / Niche.name: Stresslinux
Others / Niche.description: Stresslinux is a light-weight Linux distribution designed to test a computer's hardware by running the components at high load while monitoring their health.

Others / Niche.name: Subgraph
Others / Niche.description: Subgraph OS was a Debian-based project designed to be resistant to surveillance and interference by sophisticated adversaries over the Internet.

Others / Niche.name: Sunwah Linux
Others / Niche.description: RAYS Linux was a Chinese Debian-based Linux distribution.

Others / Niche.name: SuperGamer
Others / Niche.description: SuperGamer is a Linux distribution for the x86 platform originally based on the PCLinuxOS distribution, and is currently based on VectorLinux.

Others / Niche.name: SuperX
Others / Niche.description: SuperX is a Linux distribution, a computer operating system originally developed in India.

Others / Niche.name: T2 SDE
Others / Niche.description: The T2 SDE is an open source Linux distribution kit.

Others / Niche.name: Think Blue Linux
Others / Niche.description: Think Blue Linux is a discontinued port of Linux to IBM S/390 mainframe computers, done by the Millenux subsidiary of German company Thinking Objects Software GmbH.

Others / Niche.name: Tinfoil Hat Linux
Others / Niche.description: Tinfoil Hat Linux (THL) is a compact security-focused Linux distribution designed for high security developed by The Shmoo Group.

Others / Niche.name: Tizen
Others / Niche.description: Tizen is a Linux-based operating system primarily developed by Samsung Electronics and supported by the Linux Foundation.

Others / Niche.name: Tomsrtbt
Others / Niche.description: tomsrtbt is a very small Linux distribution.

Others / Niche.name: TopologiLinux
Others / Niche.description: TopologiLinux is a free Linux distribution to be run on an existing Microsoft Windows system.

Others / Niche.name: TOSS
Others / Niche.description: The Tri-Lab Operating System Stack (TOSS) is a Linux distribution based on Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) that was created to provide a software stack for high performance computing (HPC) clusters for laboratories within the National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA).

Others / Niche.name: Turbolinux
Others / Niche.description: Turbolinux is a discontinued Japanese Linux distribution targeting Asian users.

Others / Niche.name: Turkix
Others / Niche.description: Turkix was a live Linux distribution, capable of self-installing on hard disk using a graphical wizard.

Others / Niche.name: TurnKey Linux Virtual Appliance Library
Others / Niche.description: The TurnKey Linux Virtual Appliance Library is a free open-source software project which develops a range of Debian-based pre-packaged server software appliances.

Others / Niche.name: Twister OS
Others / Niche.description: Twister OS is a Linux-based operating system developed by Pi Labs for the Raspberry Pi series of single-board computers.

Others / Niche.name: UberStudent
Others / Niche.description: UberStudent was a free and open-source computer operating system and collection of programs aimed toward higher education and secondary students and their teachers and schools.

Others / Niche.name: Uncom OS
Others / Niche.description: Uncom OS is an open-source Linux distribution based on Ubuntu, Debian, and Arch Linux.

Others / Niche.name: UNICOS
Others / Niche.description: UNICOS is a range of Unix and later Linux operating system (OS) variants developed by Cray for its supercomputers.

Others / Niche.name: United Linux
Others / Niche.description: United Linux was an attempt by a consortium of Linux distributors to create a common base distribution for enterprise use, so as to minimize duplication of engineering effort and form an effective competitor to Red Hat.

Others / Niche.name: Unity Operating System
Others / Niche.description: Unity Operating System is a Linux distribution developed by UnionTech based on Deepin, which is based on Debian.

Others / Niche.name: Uruk GNU/Linux
Others / Niche.description: Uruk GNU/Linux-libre is a PureOS-based Linux distribution.

Others / Niche.name: UserLinux
Others / Niche.description: UserLinux was a project to create an operating system based on Debian, and targeted at business customers.

Others / Niche.name: USU
Others / Niche.description: USU linux is a Bulgarian open-source desktop operating system built around the KDE desktop environment and based on the popular free Linux distribution, Ubuntu.

Others / Niche.name: Ututo
Others / Niche.description: Ututo was a Linux distribution consisting entirely of free software.

Others / Niche.name: Vine Linux
Others / Niche.description: Vine Linux  was a Japanese Linux distribution sponsored by VineCaves.

Others / Niche.name: Vinux
Others / Niche.description: Vinux was a Linux distribution which was specially designed for blind and partially sighted users.

Others / Niche.name: Vyatta
Others / Niche.description: Vyatta is a software-based virtual router, virtual firewall and VPN product for Internet Protocol networks.

Others / Niche.name: VyOS
Others / Niche.description: VyOS is an open source network operating system Linux distribution based on Debian.

Others / Niche.name: Webconverger
Others / Niche.description: Webconverger is a discontinued Linux-based operating system designed solely for accessing Web applications privately and securely.

Others / Niche.name: Wienux
Others / Niche.description: WIENUX is a Debian-based Linux distribution developed by the City of Vienna in Austria.

Others / Niche.name: Winux
Others / Niche.description: Winux is a Linux distribution for personal computers themed close to Microsoft Windows.

Others / Niche.name: Xandros
Others / Niche.description: Xandros, Inc.

Others / Niche.name: Xandros Linux
Others / Niche.description: Xandros, Inc.

Others / Niche.name: Xbox Linux
Others / Niche.description: Xbox Linux was a project that ported the Linux operating system to the Xbox video game console.

Others / Niche.name: Xiaomi HyperOS
Others / Niche.description: Xiaomi HyperOS, sometimes shortened as HyperOS, is an operating system developed by Xiaomi.

Others / Niche.name: Yellow Dog Linux
Others / Niche.description: Yellow Dog Linux (YDL) is a discontinued free and open-source operating system for high-performance computing on multi-core processor computer architectures, focusing on GPU systems and computers using the POWER7 processor.

Others / Niche.name: Yggdrasil Linux/GNU/X
Others / Niche.description: Yggdrasil Linux/GNU/X, or LGX, is an early Linux distribution developed by Yggdrasil Computing, Incorporated, a company founded by Adam J.

Others / Niche.name: Zentyal
Others / Niche.description: Zentyal is an open source email and groupware solution based on Ubuntu Linux.

Others / Niche.name: Zeroshell
Others / Niche.description: Zeroshell is a small open-source Linux distribution for servers and embedded systems which aims to provide network services.

Others / Niche.name: ZipSlack
Others / Niche.description: ZipSlack was a specially compiled release of the Slackware Linux distribution that was designed to be lightweight and portable.

Ubuntu Family.name: Bodhi Linux
Ubuntu Family.description: Bodhi Linux is a light-weight Linux distribution based on Ubuntu that uses an Enlightenment DR17-based fork called Moksha as its desktop environment.

Ubuntu Family.name: Edubuntu
Ubuntu Family.description: Edubuntu is an official derivative of the Ubuntu operating system designed for use in classrooms inside schools, homes, and communities.

Ubuntu Family.name: Elementary OS
Ubuntu Family.description: Elementary OS is a Linux distribution based on Ubuntu LTS.

Ubuntu Family.name: KDE neon
Ubuntu Family.description: KDE neon is a Linux distribution developed by KDE based on Ubuntu long-term support (LTS) releases, bundled with a set of additional software repositories containing the latest versions of the Plasma 6 desktop environment/framework, Qt 6 toolkit and other compatible KDE software.

Ubuntu Family.name: Kubuntu
Ubuntu Family.description: Kubuntu is an official flavor of Ubuntu, a Linux distribution that uses the KDE Plasma Desktop Environment instead of the GNOME desktop environment.

Ubuntu Family.name: Linux Mint
Ubuntu Family.description: Linux Mint is a community-developed Linux distribution based on Ubuntu.

Ubuntu Family.name: Lubuntu
Ubuntu Family.description: Lubuntu is a lightweight Linux distribution based on Ubuntu that uses the LXQt desktop environment in place of GNOME.

Ubuntu Family.name: NUbuntu
Ubuntu Family.description: nUbuntu or Network Ubuntu was a project to take the existing Ubuntu operating system LiveCD and Full Installer and remaster it with tools needed for penetration testing servers and networks.

Ubuntu Family.name: Peppermint OS
Ubuntu Family.description: Peppermint OS is a Linux distribution based on Debian and Devuan Stable, and formerly based on Ubuntu.

Ubuntu Family.name: Pop! OS
Ubuntu Family.description: Pop OS is a free and open-source Linux distribution based on Ubuntu and developed by the American Linux computer manufacturer System76.

Ubuntu Family.name: Trisquel
Ubuntu Family.description: Trisquel is a computer operating system, a Linux distribution, derived from another distribution, Ubuntu.

Ubuntu Family.name: Ubuntu
Ubuntu Family.description: Ubuntu is a Linux distribution based on Debian and composed primarily of free and open-source software.

Ubuntu Family.name: Ubuntu Budgie
Ubuntu Family.description: Ubuntu Budgie is an official community flavor of Ubuntu.

Ubuntu Family.name: Ubuntu Cinnamon
Ubuntu Family.description: Ubuntu Cinnamon is a community-driven, free and open-source Linux distribution based on Ubuntu, using the Cinnamon desktop environment in place of Ubuntu's GNOME Shell.

Ubuntu Family.name: Ubuntu GNOME
Ubuntu Family.description: Ubuntu GNOME is a discontinued Linux distribution, distributed as free and open-source software.

Ubuntu Family.name: Ubuntu JeOS
Ubuntu Family.description: Ubuntu JeOS is a discontinued variant of Ubuntu Linux that is described as "an efficient variant ..

Ubuntu Family.name: Ubuntu Kylin
Ubuntu Family.description: Ubuntu Kylin is the official Chinese version of the Ubuntu computer operating system.

Ubuntu Family.name: Ubuntu MATE
Ubuntu Family.description: Ubuntu MATE is a free and open-source Linux distribution and an official derivative of Ubuntu.

Ubuntu Family.name: Ubuntu Netbook Edition
Ubuntu Family.description: Ubuntu Netbook Edition (UNE), known as Ubuntu Netbook Remix (UNR) prior to the release of Ubuntu 10.04, is a discontinued version of the Ubuntu operating system (OS) that had been optimized to enable it to work better on netbooks and other devices with small screens or with the Intel Atom CPU.

Ubuntu Family.name: Ubuntu Studio
Ubuntu Family.description: Ubuntu Studio is a recognized flavor of the Ubuntu Linux distribution, which is geared to general multimedia production.

Ubuntu Family.name: Ubuntu Touch
Ubuntu Family.description: Ubuntu Touch is a mobile version of the Ubuntu operating system, developed by the UBports community.

Ubuntu Family.name: Ubuntu Unity
Ubuntu Family.description: Ubuntu Unity is a Linux distribution based on Ubuntu, using the Unity interface in place of Ubuntu's GNOME Shell.

Ubuntu Family.name: Xubuntu
Ubuntu Family.description: Xubuntu is a Canonical-recognized, community-maintained derivative of the Ubuntu operating system.

Ubuntu Family.name: Zorin OS
Ubuntu Family.description: Zorin OS is a Linux distribution based on Ubuntu which provides both free and paid versions.

Debian Family.name: AntiX
Debian Family.description: antiX is a Linux distribution, originally based on MEPIS, which itself is based on the Debian Stable distribution.

Debian Family.name: Debian
Debian Family.description: Debian is a free, general-purpose operating system developed by the Debian Project, a worldwide association of volunteers founded by Ian Murdock in August 1993.

Debian Family.name: Debian Pure Blend
Debian Family.description: A Debian Pure Blend is a project completely inside of Debian targeting a server or a desktop installation in very broad and general terms.

Debian Family.name: Deepin
Debian Family.description: deepin is a Linux distribution, formerly based on Ubuntu and Debian, that features the Deepin Desktop Environment (DDE), a desktop environment built on Qt and available for a variety of distributions.

Debian Family.name: Devuan
Debian Family.description: Devuan is an open source, Debian-based Linux distribution that aims to maintain compatibility with other init systems and avoid lock-in by systemd.

Debian Family.name: Emdebian Grip
Debian Family.description: Emdebian Grip is a discontinued small-footprint Linux distribution based on and compatible with Debian.

Debian Family.name: Kali Linux
Debian Family.description: Kali Linux is a Linux distribution designed for digital forensics and penetration testing.

Debian Family.name: Kali NetHunter
Debian Family.description: Kali NetHunter is a free and open-source mobile penetration testing platform for Android devices, based on Kali Linux.

Debian Family.name: MX Linux
Debian Family.description: MX Linux is a midweight, desktop-oriented Linux distribution based on Debian Stable 13 "trixie" that emphasizes stability, performance, and ease of use.

Debian Family.name: Parrot OS
Debian Family.description: Parrot OS is a Linux distribution based on Debian with a focus on security, privacy, and development.

Debian Family.name: PureOS
Debian Family.description: PureOS is a Linux distribution focusing on privacy and security, using the GNOME or KDE Plasma desktop environment.

Debian Family.name: Q4OS
Debian Family.description: Q4OS is a light-weight Linux distribution based on Debian, targeted as a replacement for operating systems that are no longer supported on outdated hardware.

Debian Family.name: SolydXK
Debian Family.description: SolydXK is a Dutch Linux distribution based on Debian.

Debian Family.name: SparkyLinux
Debian Family.description: SparkyLinux is a desktop-oriented operating system based on the Debian operating system. The project offers a ready-to-use operating system with various desktops to choose from.

Red Hat / Enterprise.name: AlmaLinux
Red Hat / Enterprise.description: AlmaLinux is a free and open source Linux distribution, a community-supported, production-grade enterprise operating system that is binary-compatible with Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL).

Red Hat / Enterprise.name: CentOS
Red Hat / Enterprise.description: CentOS is a discontinued Linux distribution that provided a free and open-source community-supported computing platform, functionally compatible with its upstream source, Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL).

Red Hat / Enterprise.name: CentOS Stream
Red Hat / Enterprise.description: CentOS Stream is a community enterprise Linux distribution that exists as a midstream between the upstream development in Fedora Linux and the downstream development for Red Hat Enterprise Linux.

Red Hat / Enterprise.name: Clear Linux OS
Red Hat / Enterprise.description: Clear Linux OS is a discontinued Linux distribution, once developed and maintained on Intel's 01.org open-source platform, and optimized for Intel's microprocessors with an emphasis on performance and security.

Red Hat / Enterprise.name: ClearOS
Red Hat / Enterprise.description: ClearOS was a Linux distribution by ClearFoundation, with network gateway, file, print, mail, and messaging services.

Red Hat / Enterprise.name: Fedora Linux
Red Hat / Enterprise.description: Fedora Linux is a Linux distribution developed by the Fedora Project.

Red Hat / Enterprise.name: Oracle Linux
Red Hat / Enterprise.description: Oracle Linux is a Linux distribution packaged and freely distributed by Oracle, available partially under the GNU General Public License since late 2006.

Red Hat / Enterprise.name: Red Hat Enterprise Linux
Red Hat / Enterprise.description: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) is a commercial Linux distribution developed by Red Hat.

Red Hat / Enterprise.name: Red Hat Linux
Red Hat / Enterprise.description: Red Hat Linux was a widely used commercial open-source Linux distribution created by Red Hat until its discontinuation in 2004.

Red Hat / Enterprise.name: Rocky Linux
Red Hat / Enterprise.description: Rocky Linux is a free and open source Linux distribution developed by Rocky Enterprise Software Foundation, which is a privately owned benefit corporation that describes itself as a "self-imposed not-for-profit".

Red Hat / Enterprise.name: Russian Fedora Remix
Red Hat / Enterprise.description: Russian Fedora Remix was a remix of the Fedora Linux Linux distribution adapted for Russia that was active in 2008–2019.

Red Hat / Enterprise.name: Scientific Linux
Red Hat / Enterprise.description: Scientific Linux (SL) is a discontinued Linux distribution produced by Fermilab, CERN, DESY and by ETH Zurich.

Arch Family.name: Antergos
Arch Family.description: Antergos is a discontinued Linux distribution based on Arch Linux.

Arch Family.name: Arch Linux
Arch Family.description: Arch Linux is an open source, rolling release Linux distribution.

Arch Family.name: Arch Linux ARM
Arch Family.description: Arch Linux ARM is a port of Arch Linux for ARM processors.

Arch Family.name: ArchBang
Arch Family.description: ArchBang Linux, also briefly known as GreenBANG, is a simple lightweight rolling release Linux distribution based on a minimal Arch Linux operating system with the i3 tiling window manager, previously using the Openbox stacking window manager.

Arch Family.name: ArchLabs
Arch Family.description: ArchLabs Linux is a discontinued lightweight rolling release Linux distribution based on a minimal Arch Linux operating system with the Openbox window manager.

Arch Family.name: ArchOne
Arch Family.description: ArchOne is an Arch Linux based operating system, optimized for Acer Aspire One netbooks, but usable on other PCs with similar hardware.

Arch Family.name: Artix Linux
Arch Family.description: Artix Linux is a rolling-release Linux distribution based on Arch Linux that avoids using systemd, instead allowing users to choose between OpenRC, runit, s6, and dinit.

Arch Family.name: BlackArch
Arch Family.description: BlackArch is a penetration testing distribution based on Arch Linux that provides a large number of security tools.

Arch Family.name: EndeavourOS
Arch Family.description: EndeavourOS is a free and open-source Arch-based Linux distribution.

Arch Family.name: Garuda Linux
Arch Family.description: Garuda Linux is an Arch Linux-based, Linux distribution targeted towards gaming.

Arch Family.name: Manjaro
Arch Family.description: Manjaro is a free and open-source Linux distribution based on the Arch Linux operating system with a focus on user-friendliness, accessibility, and improved software testing and stability compared to its upstream sources.

Slackware Based.name: Absolute Linux
Slackware Based.description: Absolute Linux is a lightweight Linux distribution that works on older hardware and is based on Slackware Linux.

Slackware Based.name: BioSLAX
Slackware Based.description: BioSLAX is a Live CD, Live DVD, and Live USB operating system (OS) comprising a suite of more than 300 bioinformatics tools and application suites.

Slackware Based.name: Porteus
Slackware Based.description: Porteus is a portable operating system based on Slackware.

Slackware Based.name: Salix OS
Slackware Based.description: Salix OS is a multi-purpose Linux distribution based on Slackware.

Slackware Based.name: Slackware
Slackware Based.description: Slackware is a Linux distribution created by Patrick Volkerding in 1993.

Slackware Based.name: Slax
Slackware Based.description: Slax is a LiveCD Linux distribution developed by Tomáš Matějíček and based on upstream customizable Linux distributions.

Slackware Based.name: VectorLinux
Slackware Based.description: VectorLinux, abbreviated VL, was a Linux distribution for the x86 platform based on the Slackware Linux distribution, originally developed by Canadian developers Robert S.

Slackware Based.name: Zenwalk
Slackware Based.description: Zenwalk GNU/Linux is a desktop-focused Linux distribution founded by Jean-Philippe Guillemin.

Security / Recovery.name: BackBox
Security / Recovery.description: BackBox is a penetration test and security assessment oriented Ubuntu-based Linux distribution providing a network and informatic systems analysis toolkit.

Security / Recovery.name: CAINE Linux
Security / Recovery.description: CAINE Linux is an Italian Linux live distribution managed by Giovanni "Nanni" Bassetti.

Security / Recovery.name: Linux Kodachi
Security / Recovery.description: Linux Kodachi 9 is a derivative of Debian 13 (Trixie) focused on computer security, countering forensics and enabling anonymous browsing with minimized command-line usage designed by Warith Al Maawali.

Security / Recovery.name: Pentoo
Security / Recovery.description: Pentoo is a Live CD and Live USB designed for penetration testing and security assessment.

Security / Recovery.name: Qubes OS
Security / Recovery.description: Qubes OS is a security-focused desktop operating system that aims to provide security through compartmentalization.

Security / Recovery.name: SystemRescue
Security / Recovery.description: SystemRescue is a Linux distribution for x86-64 and IA-32 computers.

Security / Recovery.name: Tails
Security / Recovery.description: Tails, or "The Amnesic Incognito Live System", is a security-focused Debian based Linux distribution running on a computer, aimed at preserving privacy and anonymity against surveillance.

Security / Recovery.name: Whonix
Security / Recovery.description: Whonix is an anonymity focused Linux distribution.

Independent / Unique.name: KaOS
Independent / Unique.description: KaOS is an independent Linux distribution built around the latest version of the KDE Plasma desktop environment.

Independent / Unique.name: Mageia
Independent / Unique.description: Mageia is a Linux-based operating system, distributed as free and open-source software.

Independent / Unique.name: NixOS
Independent / Unique.description: NixOS is a Linux distribution built around the Nix package manager.

Independent / Unique.name: OpenMandriva Lx
Independent / Unique.description: OpenMandriva Lx is a general-purpose Linux distribution maintained by the OpenMandriva Association for x86_64 (64-bit) and ARM computers.

Independent / Unique.name: PCLinuxOS
Independent / Unique.description: PCLinuxOS, often shortened to PCLOS, is a rolling-release Linux distribution for x86-64 computers, with KDE Plasma, MATE, and XFCE as its default user interfaces.

Independent / Unique.name: Solus
Independent / Unique.description: Solus is an independently developed operating system for the x86-64 architecture based on the Linux kernel and a choice of Budgie, GNOME, KDE Plasma or Xfce as the desktop environment.

Independent / Unique.name: Void Linux
Independent / Unique.description: Void Linux is an independent Linux distribution that uses the X Binary Package System (XBPS) package manager, which was designed and implemented from scratch, and the runit init system.

Mobile / Android.name: Android
Mobile / Android.description: Android is an operating system owned by Google which is based on a modified version of the Linux kernel and other free and open-source software, designed primarily for touchscreen-based mobile devices such as smartphones and tablet computers.

Mobile / Android.name: Android-x86
Mobile / Android.description: Android-x86 is an unofficial port of the Android mobile operating system for the Intel x86 platform.

Mobile / Android.name: LineageOS
Mobile / Android.description: LineageOS is an open source Android (AOSP)-based operating system for smartphones, tablets, and set-top boxes.

Mobile / Android.name: Mobian
Mobile / Android.description: Mobian is a project to port the Debian Linux distribution running the mainline Linux kernel to smartphones and tablets.

Mobile / Android.name: PostmarketOS
Mobile / Android.description: postmarketOS is an operating system primarily for smartphones, based on the Alpine Linux distribution.

Mobile / Android.name: Sailfish OS
Mobile / Android.description: Sailfish OS is an operating system for mobile phones developed by Jolla.

Lightweight / Minimal.name: Alpine Linux
Lightweight / Minimal.description: Alpine Linux is a Linux distribution "designed for power users who appreciate security, simplicity and resource efficiency".

Lightweight / Minimal.name: LXLE Linux
Lightweight / Minimal.description: LXLE was a Linux distribution based upon the most recent Ubuntu/Lubuntu LTS release, using the LXDE desktop environment.

Lightweight / Minimal.name: Puppy Linux
Lightweight / Minimal.description: Puppy Linux is a family of light-weight Linux distributions that focus on ease of use and minimal memory footprint.

Lightweight / Minimal.name: SliTaz
Lightweight / Minimal.description: SliTaz GNU/Linux is a lightweight Linux distribution, community-based, suitable for use on older hardware and as a Live CD or Live USB.

Lightweight / Minimal.name: Tiny Core Linux
Lightweight / Minimal.description: Tiny Core Linux (TCL) is a minimal Linux kernel based operating system focusing on providing a base system using BusyBox and FLTK.

Server / Cloud / NAS.name: Koozali SME Server
Server / Cloud / NAS.description: Koozali SME server is a Linux distribution based on Red Hat Enterprise Linux and can act as a server-only or server and gateway.

Server / Cloud / NAS.name: Open Enterprise Server
Server / Cloud / NAS.description: Open Enterprise Server (OES) is a server operating system published by OpenText.

Server / Cloud / NAS.name: Proxmox Virtual Environment
Server / Cloud / NAS.description: Proxmox Virtual Environment is a virtualization platform designed for the provisioning of hyper-converged infrastructure.

Server / Cloud / NAS.name: Rancher Labs
Server / Cloud / NAS.description: Rancher Labs is an open source software company based in Cupertino, California.

Server / Cloud / NAS.name: Univention Corporate Server
Server / Cloud / NAS.description: Univention Corporate Server (UCS) is a server operating system derived from Debian with an integrated management system for the central and cross-platform administration of servers, services, clients, desktops and users as well as virtualized computers operated in UCS.

Gaming / Multimedia.name: AV Linux
Gaming / Multimedia.description: AV Linux is a Linux distribution focused on multimedia content creation.

Gaming / Multimedia.name: Lakka
Gaming / Multimedia.description: Lakka is a community-driven lightweight retro gaming Linux distribution based on LibreELEC.

Gaming / Multimedia.name: LibreELEC
Gaming / Multimedia.description: LibreELEC is an up-to-date non-profit and fully open-source fork of the OpenELEC as an up-to-date Just enough operating system.

Gaming / Multimedia.name: SteamOS
Gaming / Multimedia.description: SteamOS is a gaming-focused operating system released by Valve that incorporates the company's video game storefront, Steam.

Gentoo / Source.name: Calculate Linux
Gentoo / Source.description: Calculate Linux is a Linux distribution optimized for fast deployment in an organization environment.

Gentoo / Source.name: Sabayon Linux
Gentoo / Source.description: Sabayon Linux was an Italian Gentoo-based Linux distribution created by Fabio Erculiani and the Sabayon development team.

Gentoo / Source.name: Source Mage
Gentoo / Source.description: Source Mage is a source-based Linux distribution descended from Sorcerer.

SUSE / openSUSE.name: GeckoLinux
SUSE / openSUSE.description: GeckoLinux is a dormant Linux distribution based on openSUSE.

SUSE / openSUSE.name: OpenSUSE
SUSE / openSUSE.description: openSUSE is a free and open-source Linux distribution developed by the openSUSE Project.

SUSE / openSUSE.name: SUSE Linux Enterprise
SUSE / openSUSE.description: SUSE Linux Enterprise (SLE) is a Linux-based operating system developed by SUSE.
